one_liner: Notion offers a free, self-serve REST API with bearer-token (internal/PAT) or OAuth2 (public connection) authentication, plus both an official hosted MCP server and an open-source self-hostable MCP server, making it readily buildable for agent tooling today.

auth_methods.notes: Used for public connections (OAuth 2.0 flow) for third-party integrations.
Internal connection installation access tokens and Personal Access Tokens (PATs) are static bearer tokens created via developer portal.

self_serve_notes: Developer API access is free on any workspace plan. Internal connections/PATs can be self-serve created by workspace owners (on Free plan, only owners can create PATs by default). Public connections require OAuth app registration but are still self-serve via developer portal.

api_surface.notes: Notion REST API covers pages, databases, blocks, users, comments, search; also official JS SDK.

evidence.supports: Bearer token auth via internal connection, PAT, or OAuth for public connections
Details on internal connection static tokens vs OAuth 2.0 for public connections, self-serve via developer portal
Personal access token creation, self-serve for workspace owners
Free developer API access regardless of workspace plan

flags: PAT creation restricted to workspace owners on Free plan by default
Notion Workers/AI agent tools have separate credit-based pricing starting Aug 11, unrelated to core API
